title: The Crow and the Pitcher
content:
A crow was very thirsty. She flew over the hot, dry land looking for water. Finally, she spotted a tall pitcher sitting by the side of the road.

The crow flew down and peered inside. There was water at the bottom! But the pitcher was tall and narrow, and the crow could not reach the water with her beak.

The crow tried again and again. She was so thirsty! She sat down on the ground and began to think hard.

Then she had an idea. She saw small pebbles on the ground nearby. She picked one up in her beak and dropped it into the pitcher. Plunk! The water rose a tiny bit.

She dropped in more pebbles, one by one. Slowly, the water rose higher and higher. At last, the crow could reach the water. She drank and drank until she was no longer thirsty. Where there is a will, there is a way!

The crow flew down and peered inside. There was water at the bottom! But the pitcher was tall and narrow, and the crow could not reach the water with her beak.
The crow tried again and again. She was so thirsty! She sat down on the ground and began to think hard.
Then she had an idea. She saw small pebbles on the ground nearby. She picked one up in her beak and dropped it into the pitcher. Plunk! The water rose a tiny bit.

She dropped in more pebbles, one by one. Slowly, the water rose higher and higher. At last, the crow could reach the water. She drank and drank until she was no longer thirsty. Where there is a will, there is a way!
author: Aesop
